figures.caption: FIG. 5. Three-dimensional plots of Fermi surfaces with B = 10 Tesla. a. T = 1.2TN (above TN) , b. T = 0.8TN, c. T = 0.68TN, and d. T = 0.4TN(below TN). Color map shows the amplitude of Berry curvature on the Fermi surfaces. These plots are created using FermiSurfer [47].

FIG. 4. Symmetry and band structure of FeTe below Néel temperature. a. Magnetic Brillouin zone. b. Three symmetries under which bicollinear AFM ordering is invariant: P ⊗ T , mirror reflection with respect to the y plane My, and twofold rotation with respect to the y axis followed by half lattice translation along x and y directions C ⊗ tx/2,y/2. c. DFT band structure with bicollinear AFM ordering at T = 0. d, e. Band structures deduced from the spin-fermion model at T = 0.68TN with B = 0 and B = 10 Tesla, respectively. f, g. Band structures deduced from the spin-fermion model at T = 0.8TN with B = 0 and B = 10 Tesla, respectively. With B = 0, the three symmetries shown in b protect band crossings at the S, Y, R, and T points, as indicated by the red circles in d and f. With nonzero B, these symmetries are broken. However, there remain three other symmetries, P followed by half lattice translation along the tx/2 axis, C2y followed by half lattice translation along the y direction ty/2 and T , and My followed by tx/2 and T , which protect band crossings at the S and R points, as indicated by the red circles in e and g. In c–g, energy is measured from the nominal Fermi level EF, corresponding to 6 electrons per Fe site. In e and g, band-dependent Berry curvature is indicated by the color code, with the AHC σxy plotted against the Fermi level shift from the nominal value.

FIG. 9. Results at T = 0.7TN for B = 0 (a–c) and B = 10 Tesla (d–f). a and d show band dispersions with energy measured from the nominal Fermi level. The AHC σxy is plotted as a function of the Fermi energy shift from the nominal value. b and e (c and f) show the Fermi surface on the kz = 0 (π/c) plane.

FIG. 8. Fermi surfaces in zero external field. a and b T = 1.2TN (above TN) , c and d. T = 0.8TN, e and f. T = 0.68TN, and g and h. T = 0.4TN(below TN). Panels (a, c, e, g) show the momentum cut at kz = 0, and panels (b, d, f, g) show the momentum cut at kz = π/c.
FIG. 6. Same as in Figure 3, but with a different magnetic field B = 5 Tesla.

FIG. 7. Magnetic field-dependent AHC at various temperatures (T = 1.2TN, 0.8TN, 0.68TN and 0.4TN). Nonzero carrier doping is also considered by shifting the Fermi level from the nominal value as ∆EF = −0.02 eV, corresponding to hole doping, and ∆EF = +0.02 eV, corresponding to electron doping.
